title: ZimerfeldTree - GitFlow
skippedRemoteBranch: (skipped: the remote branch '{0}' no longer exists)
bugfixNeedsRelease: Cannot create a bugfix: there are no release branches. A bugfix can only exist tied to a release — create the release first.
bugfixSelectRelease: A bugfix can only be created tied to a release. Check 'based on' and select the source release.
releaseLocalNoRemote: Release finished locally, but no remote is configured for push.
flowErrorMissingBranch:
Git could not find the target branch (e.g. 'main' or 'develop').

Check that it exists locally:
    git branch --list main master develop

And the git flow configuration:
    git config gitflow.branch.main
    git config gitflow.branch.develop

Create the missing branch or use GitFlow Initialize.
flowErrorGeneric: The git command failed. See the details in the result window.
aboutBody:
Buttons:

  Start   — git checkout -b <prefix><name> <base>
            (default base: develop for feature/release; main for hotfix/support;
            bugfix requires a release as base — it can only exist tied to a release)

  Publish — git push --set-upstream <remote> <branch>

  Track   — git fetch + git checkout -b <branch> --track <remote>/<branch>

  Update  — git checkout <branch> + git merge <remote>/<parent> (or local if No fetch)
            (parent: develop for feature/release; main for hotfix/support; the release for bugfix)

  Finish (feature):
            git checkout <develop or based-on parent>
            git merge --no-ff <branch>
            git branch -d <branch>  (if Keep is unchecked)
            git push <remote> --delete <branch>  (if remote exists)

  Finish (bugfix):
            git checkout <release (parent), or develop if the release is gone>
            git merge --no-ff <branch>
            git branch -d <branch>  (if Keep is unchecked)
            git push <remote> --delete <branch>  (if remote exists)

  Finish (release/hotfix):
            git checkout main  →  merge --no-ff  →  tag -a <name> -m <name>
            git checkout develop  →  merge --no-ff
            git branch -d <branch>  (if Keep is unchecked)
            git push <remote> --delete <branch>  (if remote exists)
            + push main, develop, tag (release)

  Finish (support): git checkout main  →  merge --no-ff
            git branch -d <branch>  (if Keep is unchecked)
            git push <remote> --delete <branch>  (if remote exists)

Finish checkboxes:
  Keep branch after finish — skips git branch -d when finishing.
  No fetch (--no-fetch)   — does not sync with the remote before operating.

Does not require the git-flow binary — uses plain git only.
